message:
✨ Add llm-d deployment guide missions (#302)

Add 9 KB mission files for llm-d well-lit paths and features:
- Intelligent Inference Scheduling
- Prefill/Decode Disaggregation
- Wide Expert Parallelism with LeaderWorkerSet
- Tiered Prefix Cache Offloading
- Simulated Accelerators (CPU-only testing)
- Benchmark Workload
- Workload Variant Autoscaler (WVA)
- Precise Prefix Cache Routing
- Predicted Latency Scheduling (experimental)

Each mission includes install, uninstall, upgrade, and troubleshooting
sections based on the official llm-d deployment guides.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-llmd-benchmark",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "llm-d",
+  "authorGithub": "llm-d",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Run llm-d Benchmark Workload",
+    "description": "Run inference benchmarks against a deployed llm-d stack using the inference-perf tool. Supports multiple benchmark templates for different well-lit paths (inference scheduling, P/D disaggregation, wide EP, precise prefix cache). Results include latency metrics (TTFT, TPOT, ITL), throughput, and standardized benchmark reports.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Ensure llm-d stack is deployed",
+        "description": "You must have a deployed llm-d inference stack from one of the well-lit path guides:\n- [Inference Scheduling](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/inference-scheduling)\n- [P/D Disaggregation](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/pd-disaggregation)\n- [Wide EP with LWS](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/wide-ep-lws)\n- [Precise Prefix Cache](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/precise-prefix-cache-aware)"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install prerequisites",
+        "description": "Install required tools:\n- `yq` (v4+)\n- `timeout` utility (macOS: `brew install coreutils`)\n\nDownload the benchmark runner script:\n```bash\ncurl -L -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark/main/existing_stack/run_only.sh\nchmod u+x run_only.sh\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create a PVC for benchmark results",
+        "description": "Create a PersistentVolumeClaim with RWX access mode (200Gi recommended):\n```bash\nexport BENCHMARK_PVC=\"workload-pvc\"\ncat <<YAML | kubectl -n ${NAMESPACE} apply -f -\napiVersion: v1\nkind: PersistentVolumeClaim\nmetadata:\n  name: ${BENCHMARK_PVC}\nspec:\n  accessModes:\n    - ReadWriteMany\n  resources:\n    requests:\n      storage: 200Gi\nYAML\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Select and configure benchmark template",
+        "description": "Download the appropriate benchmark template for your deployment:\n```bash\n# For inference-scheduling:\ncurl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/inference_scheduling_guide_template.yaml\n\n# For P/D disaggregation:\ncurl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/pd_template.yaml\n\n# For precise prefix cache:\ncurl -LJO https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/guides/benchmark/precise_template.yaml\n```\n\nConfigure the template:\n```bash\nexport NAMESPACE=<your-namespace>\nexport BENCHMARK_PVC=workload-pvc\nexport GATEWAY_SVC=<your-gateway-service-name>\nenvsubst < <template>.yaml > config.yaml\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Run the benchmark",
+        "description": "Execute the benchmark:\n```bash\n./run_only.sh -c config.yaml\n```\n\nThe script launches a `llmdbench-harness-launcher` pod that runs inference-perf with multiple stages at different request rates. Progress is displayed in the terminal."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review benchmark results",
+        "description": "Results are stored on the PVC under `/requests/inference-perf_<experiment_ID>_<workload>_<model>/`. Key output files include:\n- `summary_lifecycle_metrics.json` — overall metrics summary\n- Per-stage YAML reports with latency percentiles (TTFT, TPOT, ITL)\n- Throughput metrics (requests/sec, tokens/sec)\n\nAccess results via the launcher pod:\n```bash\nkubectl exec -it llmdbench-harness-launcher -n ${NAMESPACE} -- ls /requests/\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful benchmark run completes all stages and produces result files on the PVC including latency metrics, throughput numbers, and standardized benchmark reports in YAML format.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "curl -L -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark/main/existing_stack/run_only.sh\nchmod u+x run_only.sh",
+        "envsubst < inference_scheduling_guide_template.yaml > config.yaml",
+        "./run_only.sh -c config.yaml"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove benchmark pod and PVC",
+        "description": "Delete the benchmark launcher pod and storage:\n```bash\nkubectl delete pod llmdbench-harness-launcher -n ${NAMESPACE}\nkubectl delete pvc ${BENCHMARK_PVC} -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Clean up local files",
+        "description": "Remove downloaded scripts and configs:\n```bash\nrm -f run_only.sh config.yaml *_template.yaml\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "upgrade": [
+      {
+        "title": "Update benchmark tooling",
+        "description": "Download the latest version of the benchmark runner:\n```bash\ncurl -L -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d-benchmark/main/existing_stack/run_only.sh\nchmod u+x run_only.sh\n```\n\nDownload updated benchmark templates from the latest llm-d release."
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Benchmark pod stuck in Pending",
+        "description": "The PVC may not have a matching PersistentVolume. Verify your storage class supports RWX access:\n```bash\nkubectl describe pvc ${BENCHMARK_PVC} -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Gateway service not found",
+        "description": "Ensure `GATEWAY_SVC` matches the exact service name in your namespace:\n```bash\nkubectl get svc -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\nCommon names: `infra-inference-scheduling-inference-gateway-istio`, `infra-pd-inference-gateway-istio`."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Benchmark failures at high request rates",
+        "description": "Request failures at high rates may indicate the stack is saturated. This is expected behavior — the benchmark sweeps through increasing rates to find the maximum throughput. Review per-stage results to identify the sustainable throughput ceiling."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "timeout command not found (macOS)",
+        "description": "On macOS, install the timeout utility:\n```bash\nbrew install coreutils\n```"
+      }
+    ]
+  },

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-llmd-inference-scheduling",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "llm-d",
+  "authorGithub": "llm-d",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install llm-d Intelligent Inference Scheduling",
+    "description": "Deploy the recommended out-of-the-box scheduling configuration for vLLM deployments using llm-d. This well-lit path reduces tail latency and increases throughput through load-aware and approximate prefix-cache-aware balancing. Defaults to 16 GPUs (8 replicas x 2 GPUs each) but supports fewer accelerators, CPU-only mode, and multiple hardware backends (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel XPU, Intel Gaudi, TPU).",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install prerequisite client tools",
+        "description": "Ensure you have the required client tools installed:\n- `helm` (v3+)\n- `helmfile` (v0.169+)\n- `kubectl` (v1.29+)\n- `yq` (v4+)\n\nSee the [client setup guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup) for detailed instructions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure Gateway API and infrastructure",
+        "description": "Ensure your cluster has:\n1. Sufficient GPU resources (16 GPUs default, or fewer with modified values)\n2. Gateway API CRDs installed\n3. A gateway provider deployed (Istio is the default)\n4. The monitoring stack installed\n\nSee the [infrastructure prerequisites](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/infrastructure) and [gateway provider guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/gateway-provider)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Create namespace and HuggingFace token secret",
+        "description": "Create a namespace for the deployment and add your HuggingFace token:\n```bash\nexport NAMESPACE=llm-d-inference-scheduler\nkubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}\n\n# Create the HuggingFace token secret\nkubectl create secret generic llm-d-hf-token \\\n  --from-literal=HF_TOKEN=<your-hf-token> \\\n  -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**Note:** Shorter namespace names are recommended to avoid Linux hostname length limitations (64 chars max)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy the stack with helmfile",
+        "description": "Navigate to the guide directory and deploy:\n```bash\ncd guides/inference-scheduling\nhelmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**For CPU-only deployment:**\n```bash\nhelmfile apply -e cpu -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**For specific gateway providers:**\n```bash\nhelmfile apply -e kgateway -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**For specific hardware backends:**\n```bash\nhelmfile apply -e amd -n ${NAMESPACE}   # AMD GPUs\nhelmfile apply -e xpu -n ${NAMESPACE}   # Intel XPU\nhelmfile apply -e hpu -n ${NAMESPACE}   # Intel Gaudi\nhelmfile apply -e gke_tpu -n ${NAMESPACE}  # Google TPU\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install HTTPRoute",
+        "description": "Apply the HTTPRoute for your gateway provider:\n\n**For Istio or kgateway:**\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**For GKE:**\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f httproute.gke.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that all 3 Helm releases are deployed:\n```bash\nhelm list -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\nExpected releases: `gaie-inference-scheduling`, `infra-inference-scheduling`, `ms-inference-scheduling`.\n\nVerify all pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\nYou should see the EPP pod, gateway pod, and 8 model service decode pods (default configuration)."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "A successful deployment shows 3 Helm releases (gaie, infra, ms) in deployed status and all pods running. The stack is ready to serve inference requests through the gateway endpoint.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "export NAMESPACE=llm-d-inference-scheduler\nkubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}",
+        "helmfile apply -n ${NAMESPACE}",
+        "kubectl apply -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}",
+        "helm list -n ${NAMESPACE}",
+        "kubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}"
+      ]
+    },
+    "uninstall": [
+      {
+        "title": "Remove the HTTPRoute",
+        "description": "Delete the HTTPRoute for your gateway provider:\n```bash\nkubectl delete -f httproute.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\nFor GKE:\n```bash\nkubectl delete -f httproute.gke.yaml -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Destroy the helmfile deployment",
+        "description": "Remove all Helm releases using helmfile:\n```bash\nhelmfile destroy -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\nOr uninstall manually:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall infra-inference-scheduling -n ${NAMESPACE} --ignore-not-found\nhelm uninstall gaie-inference-scheduling -n ${NAMESPACE}\nhelm uninstall ms-inference-scheduling -n ${NAMESPACE}\n```\n\n**Note:** If you used `$RELEASE_NAME_POSTFIX`, replace release names accordingly: `infra-$RELEASE_NAME_POSTFIX`, `gaie-$RELEASE_NAME_POSTFIX`, `ms-$RELEASE_NAME_POSTFIX`."
+      },

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "install-llmd-predicted-latency",
+  "missionClass": "install",
+  "author": "llm-d",
+  "authorGithub": "llm-d",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Install llm-d Predicted Latency Scheduling",
+    "description": "Deploy the experimental predicted latency based load balancing feature for llm-d. The Endpoint Picker (EPP) integrates with in-pod latency predictor sidecars that continuously learn from live traffic, estimating p90 TTFT and TPOT for each candidate pod. An SLO scorer compares predictions against per-request SLOs for intelligent routing. Requires building custom EPP and sidecar images from an experimental branch.",
+    "type": "deploy",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Install the Inference Gateway Extension",
+        "description": "Follow the official Gateway API Inference Extension installation:\nhttps://gateway-api-inference-extension.sigs.k8s.io/guides/\n\nEnsure you have Docker/BuildKit and access to a container registry."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Build the custom EPP image",
+        "description": "Clone and build the EPP from the experimental branch:\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension.git\ncd gateway-api-inference-extension\ngit checkout slo-prediction-experimental\n\nexport IMG=\"<your-registry>/epp:slo-prediction-$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)\"\ndocker build -t \"$IMG\" -f Dockerfile .\ndocker push \"$IMG\"\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Build the latency predictor sidecars",
+        "description": "Build the training and prediction sidecar images from the same experimental branch:\n```bash\n# See: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension/tree/slo-prediction-experimental/latencypredictor-v1\n```\n\nBuild and push training server and prediction server images to your registry."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure the plugins ConfigMap",
+        "description": "Create the `plugins-config` ConfigMap with both `default` and `slo` scheduling profiles:\n```yaml\napiVersion: inference.networking.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1\nkind: EndpointPickerConfig\nplugins:\n  - type: queue-scorer\n  - type: kv-cache-utilization-scorer\n  - type: prefix-cache-scorer\n  - type: slo-request-tracker\n  - type: slo-scorer\n  - type: slo-aware-profile-handler\n  - type: max-score-picker\n\nschedulingProfiles:\n  - name: default\n    plugins:\n      - pluginRef: slo-request-tracker\n      - pluginRef: prefix-cache-scorer\n      - pluginRef: queue-scorer\n      - pluginRef: kv-cache-utilization-scorer\n      - pluginRef: max-score-picker\n  - name: slo\n    plugins:\n      - pluginRef: prefix-cache-scorer\n        weight: 0\n      - pluginRef: slo-request-tracker\n      - pluginRef: slo-scorer\n      - pluginRef: max-score-picker\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Deploy the InferencePool with sidecars",
+        "description": "Apply your InferencePool/EPP manifest with:\n- EPP container with `--enable-latency-predictor` flag\n- Training sidecar (port 8000)\n- Three prediction sidecars (ports 8001, 8002, 8003)\n- ConfigMaps for `latency-predictor-config` and `prediction-server-config`\n\nSee the [example manifest](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension/blob/slo-prediction-experimental/config/manifests/inferencepool-resources-lp.yaml) for reference."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the deployment",
+        "description": "Check all containers are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods\n```\n\nVerify health endpoints:\n```bash\n# Training sidecar\ncurl http://<pod-ip>:8000/readyz\n\n# Prediction sidecars\ncurl http://<pod-ip>:8001/readyz\ncurl http://<pod-ip>:8002/readyz\ncurl http://<pod-ip>:8003/readyz\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Test SLO-aware routing",
+        "description": "Send requests with SLO headers to trigger predicted latency scheduling:\n```bash\ncurl -v $GW_IP/v1/completions \\\n  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n  -H 'x-prediction-based-scheduling: true' \\\n  -H 'x-slo-ttft-ms: 200' \\\n  -H 'x-slo-tpot-ms: 50' \\\n  -d '{\"model\": \"meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct\", \"prompt\": \"Hello\", \"max_tokens\": 200, \"stream\": \"true\"}'\n```\n\nThe final SSE frame includes both predictions and actuals for validation."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {

Supports multiple storage tiers: CPU RAM (recommended first tier), local disk, shared/remote storage (CephFS, GCP Lustre, IBM Storage Scale), and P2P cache sharing. Tiered caching avoids recomputation of KV tensors and SSM states, improving TTFT and throughput while reducing cost.",
+    "type": "deploy",

+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Enable CPU RAM offloading (recommended first tier)",
+        "description": "CPU RAM offloading is recommended as the first cache tier because:\n- Little operational overhead\n- Much larger capacity than GPU HBM\n- CPU-to-accelerator transfer is faster than recomputation\n\nFollow the [CPU offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/cpu) to configure CPU RAM as a cache tier in your existing helmfile values."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure local disk offloading (optional second tier)",
+        "description": "Local disk increases cache capacity but is slower than CPU RAM. Consider this when:\n- Your workload can tolerate additional latency\n- Local disk capacity is sufficient\n\nFollow the [storage offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/storage) to configure local disk as a storage backend."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure shared storage offloading (optional tier)",
+        "description": "Shared storage provides:\n- Extended capacity independent of deployment size\n- Cross-node cache sharing between replicas\n- Fast scale-up (new nodes reuse existing cache)\n- Persistence across restarts and failures\n\nSupported storage systems: CephFS, GCP Lustre, IBM Storage Scale, and any vLLM-compatible storage connector.\n\nFollow the [storage offloading guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/tiered-prefix-cache/storage)."

+      }

Prometheus monitoring stack with HTTPS support\n\n**Platform-specific Prometheus setup:**\n- **OpenShift:** Enable User Workload Monitoring\n- **GKE:** Install in-cluster Prometheus (GMP lacks HTTP API)\n- **Kind/Minikube:** Install Prometheus with TLS configuration\n\nSee [monitoring documentation](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/docs/monitoring) for details."
